name: Grady Tate
abstract: Grady Tate (January 14, 1932 – October 8, 2017) was an American jazz and soul-jazz drummer and baritone vocalist. In addition to his work as sideman, Tate released many albums as leader and lent his voice to songs in the animated Schoolhouse Rock!

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker
about_quotes.evidence: I actually played on one of his solo records where he was the main vocalist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chris Parker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 5, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel
about_quotes.evidence: See I play with Grady Tate and he’s a singer, and lightly. I don’t even have to put my hand on the conga, the full hand on it. I just do my fingertips just to get a little sound, enough to back him up without overpowering him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: And then I’ve been working with another group called Lionel Hampton and the Golden Men of Jazz... usually it’s... Grady Tate usually on drums
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Roland Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: I had a group called the New York Jazz Quartet at that time... Grady [Tate] was still wailing away... we all walked off and left him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roland Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Grady Tate, Buddy Tate, James Moody, Clark Terry, Al Gray and myself, Lionel Hampton. So, we’re all bandleaders in there
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: I did Grady Tate’s first vocal album “Windmills of your Mind.” I did one side and Gary did one side.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003
